label: Wallet Infrastructure
polygonRole: Embedded user wallets, institutional wallet policy, delegated signing, custody partner routing, and account abstraction patterns under one OMS integration.

providers.name: Circle Wallets
providers.pricingSignal: 1,000 MAW free; signing-only and all-included MAW tiers published.
providers.strength: USDC-native wallet primitives and Gas Station.
providers.tradeoff: Best when Circle is already the stablecoin/control plane.

providers.name: Coinbase CDP
providers.pricingSignal: 5,000 wallet operations free, then public per-operation pricing.
providers.strength: Developer-friendly embedded wallet and Base ecosystem fit.
providers.tradeoff: Wallet operation pricing can grow with high-frequency use.

providers.name: Crossmint
providers.pricingSignal: 1,000 wallets free; wallet overage public; payments custom/tiered.
providers.strength: Good consumer onboarding and NFT/digital commerce paths.
providers.tradeoff: Broad platform rather than financial-institution money rail.

providers.name: Dynamic
providers.pricingSignal: Free tier and growth plan published.
providers.strength: Excellent login, embedded wallet UX, and multi-wallet support.
providers.tradeoff: Not a full money movement stack.

providers.name: Privy
providers.pricingSignal: Public pricing tiers plus enterprise plan.
providers.strength: Strong embedded wallet UX for apps starting from zero.
providers.tradeoff: Needs other providers for ramps, settlement, compliance, and cross-border payout.

providers.name: Turnkey
providers.pricingSignal: Free signature allowance; per-signature public model.
providers.strength: Excellent secure signing and policy engine.
providers.tradeoff: Infrastructure primitive, not orchestration.

providers.name: Fireblocks
providers.pricingSignal: Starter and custom annual pricing published.
providers.strength: Institutional security, policy approvals, and operations.
providers.tradeoff: Enterprise-heavy and one piece of the overall stack.

providers.name: DFNS
providers.strength: Bank-grade wallet infrastructure and key management.
providers.tradeoff: Pricing and implementation depend on enterprise scope.

providers.name: Fordefi
providers.strength: Institutional MPC and transaction policy workflows.
providers.tradeoff: Requires separate money movement and ramp orchestration.

providers.name: Safe
providers.strength: Mature multisig and smart account ecosystem.
providers.tradeoff: Less turnkey for regulated fintech flows.

providers.name: Sequence
providers.pricingSignal: Free up to 499 MAU and 50K signatures; Core $299/mo to 2.5K MAU; enterprise ~$0.001 per signature.
providers.strength: Production-scale Trails intents engine (10M+ tx in <2 months, 2x conversion lift), embedded wallets, Google Cloud partnership.
providers.tradeoff: Now part of the Polygon Open Money Stack via the Jan 2026 Coinme+Sequence acquisition; commercial terms via Polygon enterprise.

providers.name: Web3Auth
providers.strength: Fast social login and wallet abstraction.
providers.tradeoff: Needs separate compliance and stablecoin rails.

providers.name: ZeroDev
providers.strength: Smart-account developer experience.
providers.tradeoff: Developer primitive rather than financial stack.

providers.name: Biconomy
providers.strength: Gas abstraction and smart-account tooling.
providers.tradeoff: Complements OMS rather than replacing it.

providers.name: thirdweb
providers.strength: Broad app primitives, wallets, contracts, infra.
providers.tradeoff: Not tailored to regulated financial institutions.

label: Crosschain Interoperability
polygonRole: AggLayer-style liquidity, crosschain settlement, message routing, and asset movement between institution-owned systems and public chains.

providers.name: Chainlink CCIP
providers.strength: Security reputation and enterprise adoption.
providers.tradeoff: Separate integration and dynamic route costs.

providers.name: LayerZero
providers.pricingSignal: Executor, DVN, and destination gas quoting.
providers.strength: Broad chain coverage and developer adoption.
providers.tradeoff: Requires message security configuration decisions.

providers.name: Wormhole
providers.pricingSignal: Relayer and gas costs vary by route.
providers.strength: Large ecosystem and mature asset transfer paths.
providers.tradeoff: Operational risk shifts into bridge/relayer choices.

providers.name: Axelar
providers.pricingSignal: Gas and relayer fee quote model.
providers.strength: Interchain messaging plus developer tooling.
providers.tradeoff: Another network dependency for treasury teams.

providers.name: Across
providers.pricingSignal: Dynamic relayer fee plus LP fee model.
providers.strength: Fast crosschain liquidity settlement.
providers.tradeoff: Route-specific liquidity and fee behavior.

providers.name: LI.FI
providers.pricingSignal: Aggregator fee and partner route fees.
providers.strength: Aggregates bridge and DEX routes.
providers.tradeoff: Great routing layer, not a unified compliance stack.

providers.name: Socket
providers.strength: Developer-friendly bridge aggregation.
providers.tradeoff: Requires separate treasury/compliance controls.

providers.name: Squid
providers.strength: Crosschain liquidity via Axelar ecosystem.
providers.tradeoff: Point solution for swaps and routes.

providers.name: Hyperlane
providers.strength: Configurable interchain security model.
providers.tradeoff: More engineering ownership.

providers.name: deBridge
providers.strength: Fast crosschain value transfer.
providers.tradeoff: Needs broader financial ops integration.

providers.name: Celer
providers.strength: Bridge and message passing coverage.
providers.tradeoff: Additional bridge risk surface.

providers.name: Stargate
providers.strength: Liquidity movement for supported assets.
providers.tradeoff: Asset and pool availability constraints.

providers.name: Router Protocol
providers.strength: Crosschain intent and message routing.
providers.tradeoff: Less directly institution-packaged than OMS.

providers.name: Agglayer
providers.pricingSignal: FREE connection for CDK chains; usage-based fees payable in any token; only network gas today.
providers.strength: Pessimistic proofs (mathematically secure even if a connected chain is compromised), unified liquidity across connected chains, no rent-seeking, settlement bundling lowers L1 cost.
providers.tradeoff: Maximum value paired with a CDK chain; for existing chains it behaves as an interop layer alongside LayerZero/CCIP.

providers.name: Sequence Trails
providers.pricingSignal: Tiered with Sequence wallet plan; enterprise per-action quote.
providers.strength: 1-click cross-chain abstraction (bridge + swap + gas hidden from user); 10M+ tx in <2 months; 2x conversion lift vs. non-Trails wallets.
providers.tradeoff: Best with Sequence wallets; integrated into Polygon Open Money Stack post-acquisition.

label: Stablecoin Orchestration
polygonRole: Issue, hold, swap, settle, reconcile, and route stablecoins across wallets, merchants, agent wallets, institutions, and local payout partners.

providers.name: Bridge
providers.pricingSignal: Gas pass-through, partner charges, and disclosed FX spread up to 1%.
providers.strength: Stablecoin-native account and orchestration APIs.
providers.tradeoff: Can become another platform beside wallets, ramps, and compliance.

providers.name: BVNK
providers.pricingSignal: Typical provider ranges cited around 0.3%-2%; enterprise custom.
providers.strength: Merchant and treasury stablecoin workflows.
providers.tradeoff: Less focused on bespoke chain infrastructure.

providers.name: Circle Mint and CPN
providers.pricingSignal: Enterprise access/custom; wallet fees public separately.
providers.strength: USDC issuer, mint/redeem, and institutional network credibility.
providers.tradeoff: Circle-first asset and network strategy.

providers.name: ZeroHash
providers.pricingSignal: Platform pricing custom; issuer fee schedules published for some stablecoins.
providers.strength: Regulated crypto infrastructure for fintechs.
providers.tradeoff: Full commercials require sales process.

providers.name: Stripe Stablecoin Payments
providers.pricingSignal: Stablecoin payments public signal around 1.5% where available.
providers.strength: Stripe distribution and merchant tooling.
providers.tradeoff: Payments acceptance, not full OMS replacement.

providers.name: Coinbase Payments
providers.pricingSignal: Onramp and commerce fees vary by product.
providers.strength: Consumer crypto distribution and Base ecosystem.
providers.tradeoff: Coinbase-centered rails.

providers.name: Rain
providers.strength: Stablecoin card issuing and spend programs.
providers.tradeoff: Specific issuing use case.

providers.name: Mural
providers.strength: Global vendor payments and accounts payable.
providers.tradeoff: Business app layer, not infrastructure layer.

providers.name: Cedar
providers.strength: Treasury and cross-border business payments.
providers.tradeoff: Narrower orchestration surface.

providers.name: Brale
providers.strength: Stablecoin issuance and compliance workflows.
providers.tradeoff: Issuer-specific implementation scope.

providers.name: Paxos
providers.strength: Regulated issuer and institutional credibility.
providers.tradeoff: Commercials and product scope require enterprise deal.

providers.name: Agora
providers.strength: Stablecoin issuance/network focus.
providers.tradeoff: Emerging platform.

providers.name: M0
providers.strength: Modular stablecoin infrastructure.
providers.tradeoff: Different thesis than Polygon one-integration OMS.

label: Cash Ramps and On/Off-Ramp
polygonRole: Fiat access into and out of stablecoin rails through partner ramps, local payout methods, cash-in/cash-out paths, and orchestration controls.

providers.name: MoonPay
providers.pricingSignal: Direct fees up to 4.5%; other product fees vary.
providers.strength: Large consumer ramp footprint.
providers.tradeoff: Consumer-heavy UX and higher visible fees.

providers.name: Ramp Network
providers.pricingSignal: Bank up to 1.4%; cards up to 3.9%-5.45% by region/product.
providers.strength: Broad ramp coverage and widget/API options.
providers.tradeoff: Route fees vary by geography and payment method.

providers.name: Transak
providers.pricingSignal: Off-ramp public signal around 1%; on-ramp route-dependent.
providers.strength: Wide asset and country coverage.
providers.tradeoff: Consumer KYC and regional availability constraints.

providers.name: Coinbase Onramp
providers.pricingSignal: No-fee USDC on Base available by request in some contexts; product pricing varies.
providers.strength: Brand trust and Coinbase user base.
providers.tradeoff: Coinbase account and ecosystem dependency.

providers.name: Banxa
providers.strength: Global payment methods and crypto ramp coverage.
providers.tradeoff: Commercials often partner-specific.

providers.name: Onramper
providers.pricingSignal: Public plans start around $199/mo and $599/mo premium.
providers.strength: Aggregates multiple ramp providers.
providers.tradeoff: Adds aggregator layer rather than reducing vendors.

providers.name: Sardine
providers.strength: Fraud, compliance, and crypto ramp controls.
providers.tradeoff: Custom enterprise pricing.

providers.name: Mercuryo
providers.strength: On/off-ramp and payments footprint.
providers.tradeoff: Coverage and fees vary by region.

providers.name: Alchemy Pay
providers.strength: Global payment method coverage.
providers.tradeoff: Integration still separate from wallet/settlement stack.

providers.name: Simplex/Nuvei
providers.strength: Card payment processing and ramp heritage.
providers.tradeoff: Payment-processor style commercials.

providers.name: Mesh
providers.strength: Account connectivity and transfer UX.
providers.tradeoff: More account linking than full ramp stack.

providers.name: Unlimit
providers.strength: Global acquiring and payout footprint.
providers.tradeoff: Broad payments provider, not Polygon-native.

providers.name: Coinme
providers.pricingSignal: Retail kiosk ~5% + $2.95–4.74; B2B custom (1–2.5% negotiated typical).
providers.strength: 48-state US money transmitter licenses, 50,000+ retail locations, MSB-registered, 1M+ existing users; closes the US fiat regulatory gap in the OMS.
providers.tradeoff: Now part of the Polygon Open Money Stack; commercial terms via Polygon enterprise. International ramps still partner-led.

label: Cross-Border Payments
polygonRole: Stablecoin settlement with fiat endpoints, local payment methods, liquidity routing, and faster reconciliation across corridors.

providers.name: Wise Platform
providers.pricingSignal: Public business pricing and upfront fee calculator.
providers.strength: Transparent FX and strong consumer/business brand.
providers.tradeoff: Fiat rails first, not programmable stablecoin settlement.

providers.name: Airwallex
providers.pricingSignal: Public pricing pages; some platform features custom.
providers.strength: Multi-currency accounts, cards, and global payments.
providers.tradeoff: Financial platform competitor, not chain infrastructure.

providers.name: Nium
providers.pricingSignal: Custom; markets 190+ countries and 100+ real-time corridors.
providers.strength: Bank-grade payout network and APIs.
providers.tradeoff: Quote-based and fiat-network heavy.

providers.name: Thunes
providers.strength: Large cross-border payout network.
providers.tradeoff: Network layer, not stablecoin architecture layer.

providers.name: Rapyd
providers.pricingSignal: Regional pricing pages and custom enterprise.
providers.strength: Payments, payouts, wallets, and issuing breadth.
providers.tradeoff: Many-product platform with region-specific complexity.

providers.name: dLocal
providers.strength: Local payments and payouts in emerging markets.
providers.tradeoff: Corridor-specific rails rather than one onchain orchestration layer.

providers.name: TerraPay
providers.strength: Global payout network and remittance coverage.
providers.tradeoff: Network access does not solve wallet/stablecoin/CDK layers.

providers.name: Payoneer
providers.pricingSignal: Public and custom pricing by product.
providers.strength: SMB and marketplace payout footprint.
providers.tradeoff: Less programmable onchain infrastructure.

providers.name: Visa Direct
providers.strength: Global card network reach.
providers.tradeoff: Card rails constraints and intermediaries.

providers.name: Mastercard Move
providers.strength: Large network, cards, accounts, wallets.
providers.tradeoff: Network-led, not open stablecoin stack.

providers.name: Worldpay
providers.strength: Large acquiring and merchant settlement network.
providers.tradeoff: Merchant acquiring first.

providers.name: Flutterwave
providers.strength: African payment method coverage.
providers.tradeoff: Region-focused fiat payments.

providers.name: Tazapay
providers.strength: Escrow and cross-border merchant payments.
providers.tradeoff: Specific B2B commerce focus.

providers.name: Toku
providers.strength: Latin American recurring payments and local methods.
providers.tradeoff: Complementary local collection layer.

label: Blockchain Integration
polygonRole: RPC, indexers, explorers, smart contract tooling, webhooks, event monitoring, and settlement observability inside the OMS implementation.

providers.name: Alchemy
providers.strength: Strong developer tooling and APIs.
providers.tradeoff: Infra platform rather than money-stack orchestration.

providers.name: QuickNode
providers.strength: Broad chain RPC and marketplace.
providers.tradeoff: Needs surrounding wallet, ramp, and compliance stack.

providers.name: Infura
providers.strength: Mature Ethereum infrastructure.
providers.tradeoff: Infrastructure point solution.

providers.name: Chainstack
providers.strength: Node deployment and enterprise support.
providers.tradeoff: Does not replace stablecoin/payment operations.

providers.name: Blockdaemon
providers.strength: Institutional nodes, staking, API infrastructure.
providers.tradeoff: Enterprise infra, not one-integration OMS.

providers.name: Tenderly
providers.strength: Contract simulation, alerts, and debugging.
providers.tradeoff: Developer operations layer only.

providers.name: Blockscout
providers.strength: Explorer and transaction transparency.
providers.tradeoff: Observability, not settlement orchestration.

providers.name: The Graph
providers.strength: Decentralized indexing.
providers.tradeoff: Requires subgraph engineering.

providers.name: Goldsky
providers.strength: Realtime blockchain data pipelines.
providers.tradeoff: Complements settlement workflows.

providers.name: Moralis
providers.strength: Fast wallet/token/NFT APIs.
providers.tradeoff: App data layer rather than fintech rail.

providers.name: Covalent
providers.strength: Historical and multichain data coverage.
providers.tradeoff: Analytics API, not money movement.

label: Blockchain-as-a-service (CDK)
polygonRole: Bespoke institutional chains, appchains, compliance-aware settlement environments, and AggLayer-connected liquidity using Polygon CDK-style deployment.

providers.name: Polygon CDK + Agglayer
providers.pricingSignal: Open source (MIT/Apache); Agglayer connection FREE for CDK chains; no mandatory revenue share.
providers.strength: Polygon-native bespoke chain with built-in Agglayer unified liquidity; 20K TPS at ~$0.003/tx; no rent-seeking vs. OP Stack (2.5% rev) or Arbitrum Orbit (10% net rev).
providers.tradeoff: Enterprise/managed deployments are commercial; self-hosted infra still ~$5–15K/mo depending on DA layer choice.

providers.name: AvaCloud
providers.pricingSignal: Published testnet, mainnet, and pro monthly plans.
providers.strength: Clear RaaS packaging and Avalanche ecosystem.
providers.tradeoff: Avalanche-specific chain strategy.

providers.name: OP Stack and Superchain
providers.pricingSignal: Open source; ecosystem and service costs vary.
providers.strength: Large ecosystem and Superchain narrative.
providers.tradeoff: Operationalization and service providers still needed.

providers.name: Arbitrum Orbit
providers.pricingSignal: Open framework; provider and DAO/licensing considerations vary.
providers.strength: Strong Ethereum scaling ecosystem.
providers.tradeoff: Commercial path depends on deployment model.

providers.name: zkSync ZK Stack
providers.strength: ZK chain thesis and Elastic Chain direction.
providers.tradeoff: Institutional packaging less direct than a managed OMS story.

providers.name: Cosmos SDK and IBC
providers.strength: Mature appchain model and IBC.
providers.tradeoff: Higher protocol ownership.

providers.name: Substrate/Polkadot
providers.strength: Custom runtime control.
providers.tradeoff: Specialized engineering requirements.

providers.name: Caldera
providers.strength: Managed rollup deployment.
providers.tradeoff: RaaS point provider.

providers.name: Conduit
providers.strength: Managed OP/Arbitrum-style rollups.
providers.tradeoff: Needs separate money movement product layer.

providers.name: Gelato RaaS
providers.strength: Managed chains plus automation tooling.
providers.tradeoff: RaaS plus integrations, not whole OMS.

providers.name: Alchemy Rollups
providers.strength: Developer platform plus rollup launch.
providers.tradeoff: Developer infra orientation.

label: Compliance and Security Layer
polygonRole: Policy orchestration, compliance hooks, transaction monitoring, risk scoring, sanctions workflows, velocity limits, ledger/audit trails, and incident controls around OMS flows.

providers.name: Chainalysis
providers.strength: Sanctions, KYT, investigations, institutional trust.
providers.tradeoff: Specialized analytics provider.

providers.name: TRM Labs
providers.strength: Risk scoring, investigations, compliance coverage.
providers.tradeoff: Must be integrated into money movement workflows.

providers.name: Elliptic
providers.strength: Crypto compliance and risk intelligence.
providers.tradeoff: Point compliance layer.

providers.name: Notabene
providers.strength: Travel Rule network and VASP workflows.
providers.tradeoff: Specific regulatory control.

providers.name: Sardine
providers.strength: Fraud, device, KYC, crypto risk controls.
providers.tradeoff: Custom enterprise implementation.

providers.name: Alloy
providers.strength: KYC/KYB orchestration and decisioning.
providers.tradeoff: Identity layer, not settlement layer.

providers.name: Persona
providers.strength: KYC flows and document verification UX.
providers.tradeoff: Needs policy and transaction monitoring around it.

providers.name: Sumsub
providers.strength: Global verification and KYB/KYC modules.
providers.tradeoff: Verification provider only.

providers.name: ComplyAdvantage
providers.strength: Sanctions, PEP, adverse media screening.
providers.tradeoff: Screening layer.

providers.name: Unit21
providers.strength: Case management and transaction monitoring.
providers.tradeoff: Ops layer must connect to rails.

providers.name: Blockaid
providers.strength: Transaction simulation and malicious interaction protection.
providers.tradeoff: Security control, not compliance program.

providers.name: Hypernative
providers.strength: Realtime threat detection and incident monitoring.
providers.tradeoff: Runtime security layer.

providers.name: Polygon ID / Privado ID
providers.pricingSignal: Open source; infra/hosting cost only (~$10–50K/yr at scale); no per-verification fees.
providers.strength: Zero-knowledge proof-based credentials, protocol-agnostic, credentials marketplace model (issuers pay verifiers, not users); large savings vs. $0.80–2.50 per check vendors at scale.
providers.tradeoff: Newer than traditional KYC vendors; best as a complement to a regulated KYC partner (Sumsub/Persona/Veriff) for the regulated verification moment itself, with Polygon ID handling reusable credentials thereafter.

label: Settlement Chain
polygonRole: Polygon PoS as the lowest-cost, battle-tested EVM settlement layer with 5-second finality and 99.99% uptime. The OMS abstracts the chain choice but the chain economics define unit cost.

providers.name: Polygon PoS
providers.pricingSignal: $0.001–0.002 per transaction; 5-second finality; 3,200+ TPS actual; 99.99% uptime since 2020.
providers.strength: Lowest unit cost among major EVM chains, predictable gas after recent hardfork, battle-tested at scale, native Agglayer + Vaultbridge + CDK integration.
providers.tradeoff: Sidechain security model differs from Ethereum L1; for purist L1-equivalent security, see Polygon zkEVM.

providers.name: Polygon zkEVM
providers.pricingSignal: ~$0.015 per transaction; finality <2.5 seconds via ZK proofs.
providers.strength: EVM equivalence with Ethereum-derived security via ZK validity proofs; settles to Ethereum.
providers.tradeoff: 7-15x more expensive than Polygon PoS; only choose when L1-equivalent security is non-negotiable.

providers.name: Base (Coinbase L2)
providers.pricingSignal: ~$0.09 per transaction; 2-sec L2 finality; 7-day L1 challenge window.
providers.strength: Fastest-growing L2, deep Coinbase integration, free USDC on-ramp from Coinbase users.
providers.tradeoff: 45–90x more expensive per tx than Polygon PoS; 7-day fraud-proof window for L1 exits; Coinbase-controlled sequencer.

providers.name: Arbitrum One
providers.pricingSignal: ~$0.09 per transaction; TimeBoost MEV auction; 7-day challenge window.
providers.strength: Highest L2 TVL, mature Stage 1 rollup, Arbitrum Orbit for L3s.
providers.tradeoff: 45–90x more expensive per tx than Polygon PoS; Orbit independent chains owe 10% net protocol revenue.

providers.name: Optimism
providers.strength: Superchain ecosystem (Base, World, Unichain), strong governance.
providers.tradeoff: Superchain members owe 2.5% of revenue OR 15% of profit to OP Collective; 45–90x more expensive than Polygon PoS.

providers.name: Solana
providers.pricingSignal: $0.0005–0.0018 per tx + priority fees; ~480ms finality; 5,500 actual TPS.
providers.strength: Sub-second finality, highest throughput, growing stablecoin volume.
providers.tradeoff: Non-EVM (requires Rust/Anchor rebuild), historic uptime issues (multiple multi-hour outages), Squads multisig instead of Safe.

providers.name: Stellar
providers.pricingSignal: ~$0.0008 per tx; 5–6 second finality; built-in DEX for FX.
providers.strength: Built specifically for payments; MoneyGram, Circle USDC native integration.
providers.tradeoff: Limited DeFi/composability vs. EVM ecosystem; non-EVM (Soroban smart contracts new).

providers.name: Tron
providers.strength: 75%+ of global USDT transfer volume; massive retail liquidity in emerging markets.
providers.tradeoff: Highest unit cost among major chains; centralization concerns; regulatory scrutiny on Tether dependence.

providers.name: Avalanche C-Chain
providers.strength: EVM-compatible, subnets for app-specific chains, 4,500 TPS.
providers.tradeoff: 25–250x more expensive than Polygon PoS; less stablecoin liquidity than Polygon/Tron/Solana.

providers.name: BNB Chain
providers.strength: Massive Asia-Pacific user base, zero-fee stablecoin program through March 2026.
providers.tradeoff: Centralization concerns (21 validators), Binance-aligned governance, regulatory exposure.

providers.name: Ethereum Mainnet
providers.pricingSignal: <$0.01 (May 2026 lows) to several dollars under load; 12-sec blocks, ~13-min finality.
providers.strength: Most decentralized, deepest L1 security, settlement layer for all major L2s.
providers.tradeoff: Variable cost (gas spikes destroy unit economics for micropayments); slow finality vs. Polygon PoS 5-sec.

providers.name: Sui
providers.strength: Parallel execution, object-centric model, sub-second finality.
providers.tradeoff: Non-EVM (Move language); ecosystem still maturing.

providers.name: Aptos
providers.pricingSignal: <$0.01 per tx; <1-sec finality; all fees burned.
providers.strength: Block-STM parallel execution, low latency.
providers.tradeoff: Non-EVM, smaller stablecoin liquidity, newer ecosystem.

label: Yield & Treasury
polygonRole: Polygon Vaultbridge turns idle bridged TVL into chain-level revenue — a unique mechanism no other L2 framework offers. Compare against external yield protocols that the chain or builder would otherwise route to.

providers.name: Polygon Vaultbridge
providers.pricingSignal: Morpho-powered vaults; chain captures yield from idle TVL (Katana: $230M+ deposited → $2M+ revenue distributed).
providers.strength: Unique to Polygon's stack: bridged assets earn 4–8% APY that flows to the chain operator, not just individual holders; supports ETH/USDC/USDT/WBTC + custom tokens; vbToken 1:1 wrappers tradeable on destination chain.
providers.tradeoff: Chain-level revenue mechanism; chain operator decides distribution policy (ecosystem incentives, user rewards, treasury). Conservative curated strategies (Gauntlet/Steakhouse).

providers.name: Morpho
providers.strength: 50+ curators (Gauntlet, Steakhouse, etc.); the protocol that powers Vaultbridge.
providers.tradeoff: Standalone integration means manual vault selection and curator management; Vaultbridge automates this.

providers.name: Aave V3
providers.pricingSignal: ~10% reserve factor on borrow interest; 3–5% APY on USDC/USDT supply.
providers.strength: Largest lending TVL, established risk frameworks, $50M/year buyback program.
providers.tradeoff: Lower yield than curated Morpho vaults; less customization for institutional deposits.

providers.name: Compound V3
providers.pricingSignal: ~10% reserve factor; algorithmic rates; 3–5% APY USDC.
providers.strength: Battle-tested, COMP rewards, simple integration.
providers.tradeoff: Limited asset support per market; lower yields than concentrated vaults.

providers.name: Spark (Sky/MakerDAO)
providers.pricingSignal: Up to 6% APY via governance-defined rates; via borrow interest.
providers.strength: Backed by Sky's USDS reserves; deep liquidity for stablecoin deposits.
providers.tradeoff: Rates governance-controlled rather than algorithmic; Maker/Sky-ecosystem-aligned.

providers.name: Yearn Finance
providers.strength: Auto-compounding, aggressive strategy diversification.
providers.tradeoff: Higher fees than direct vaults; strategy risk requires monitoring.

providers.name: Pendle
providers.pricingSignal: 5% protocol fee on yield accrued; $3.5B TVL.
providers.strength: Yield tokenization (PT/YT split) for fixed-yield or speculative positioning.
providers.tradeoff: Speculative layer on top of base yield; not a treasury primitive.

label: Card Issuing & BaaS
polygonRole: Polygon's stack handles settlement and stablecoin movement, but neobanks need card programs for consumer touchpoint and interchange revenue (1.6–2% gross). Pair Polygon for the backend with a card issuer for the front end.

providers.name: Stripe Issuing + Treasury
providers.pricingSignal: $0.10 per virtual card; $3.50 physical; no monthly minimum; interchange revenue share available.
providers.strength: Lowest setup friction, deep payments stack integration.
providers.tradeoff: US-centric; interchange share opaque; not a programmable settlement layer.

providers.name: Marqeta
providers.pricingSignal: Custom; typical enterprise minimums $100K+/yr; 100% interchange capture with client share.
providers.strength: Powers Coinbase Card, Square Cash App, Klarna; most flexible programmable cards.
providers.tradeoff: Enterprise-only effective minimums; complex integration.

providers.name: Lithic
providers.pricingSignal: Per-card pricing not public; no expensive monthly minimums; interchange negotiated.
providers.strength: Developer-friendly API, fast deploy for startup card programs.
providers.tradeoff: Smaller scale than Marqeta; fewer enterprise features.

providers.name: Unit.co
providers.pricingSignal: Custom (default $0 monthly); 70% of net interchange to fintech.
providers.strength: Full banking stack (accounts, cards, ACH, wires) with strong interchange terms.
providers.tradeoff: US-only; partner bank dependency.

providers.name: Galileo (SoFi)
providers.strength: Mature processor; powers Robinhood, Chime historically.
providers.tradeoff: Enterprise-only; SoFi-owned (competitor concerns).

providers.name: Column
providers.pricingSignal: Monthly minimum + per-transaction; 100% interchange to program manager before bank fees; ~$0.50 ACH, ~$5 wire.
providers.strength: Direct bank charter — no intermediary BaaS layer; full interchange capture.
providers.tradeoff: Smaller scale; requires more compliance ownership.

providers.name: Treasury Prime
providers.pricingSignal: Monthly fee + usage; 50–90% interchange share based on volume.
providers.strength: Multi-bank network gives redundancy and rate-shopping.
providers.tradeoff: Adds vendor layer between fintech and bank.

providers.name: Highnote
providers.strength: Modern API with embedded ledger; growing fintech adoption.
providers.tradeoff: Newer; smaller customer base than Marqeta/Galileo.

providers.name: Modern Treasury
providers.strength: Best-in-class payment ops layer (reconciliation, ledger) across ACH/wire/RTP.
providers.tradeoff: Ops layer, not issuer — pair with another card program.

label: Identity & KYC
polygonRole: Polygon ID provides open-source ZK-based reusable credentials (no per-verification fees). Pair it with a regulated KYC vendor (Sumsub/Persona/Veriff) for the initial regulated verification moment, then issue reusable Polygon ID credentials for downstream re-use. This is where Polygon's open-source position translates to real cost reduction at scale.

providers.name: Polygon ID / Privado ID
providers.pricingSignal: Open source; infra/hosting only (~$10–50K/yr at scale); no per-verification fees.
providers.strength: Zero-knowledge proof credentials; reusable across apps and chains; no per-check pricing; protocol-agnostic.
providers.tradeoff: Complement, not replacement, for the regulated KYC moment itself.

providers.name: Sumsub
providers.strength: 14-day free trial (50 checks); global document coverage; KYB modules.
providers.tradeoff: Per-verification cost compounds quickly at scale ($1.35 × 100K = $135K/yr).

providers.name: Persona
providers.pricingSignal: Essential $250/mo+; startup program offers 500 free/mo for eligible.
providers.strength: Polished UX; flexible workflow builder; strong document coverage.
providers.tradeoff: Enterprise pricing opaque; total cost driven by verification volume.

providers.name: Veriff
providers.strength: Biometric analysis included; tiered self-serve up to enterprise.
providers.tradeoff: Per-check pricing similar to Sumsub at scale.

providers.name: Onfido
providers.strength: Enterprise-focused; strong fraud/AI detection.
providers.tradeoff: Quote-only; no published pricing.

providers.name: Jumio
providers.strength: Premium expert review; high-trust regulated industries.
providers.tradeoff: Highest-cost tier of major KYC vendors.

providers.name: Civic
providers.pricingSignal: Variable; $0.05/month per active pass; marketplace pricing.
providers.strength: Web3-native; reusable Pass model; low per-user cost.
providers.tradeoff: Less regulated-finance penetration than Sumsub/Persona.

providers.name: Alloy
providers.strength: Orchestrates multiple KYC vendors with decisioning rules; mature in regulated fintech.
providers.tradeoff: Orchestration layer, not a verification provider — adds cost on top of underlying vendors.
